mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on directional trend effect independent of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low (a Sahm-rule-like threshold), it signals an emerging recession, causing equity markets to price in earnings declines and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest value 3.9 near historic lows
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where the UNRATE threshold is triggered, measured over a 5-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration of RSAFS (retail sales) and INDPRO (industrial production) indicates synchronized demand and supply-side expansion, reinforcing corporate revenue and margin expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month. Neither series alone captures this reinforcing effect; only their joint positive momentum does.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and INDPRO 3-month annualized growth rates are positive simultaneously
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint RSAFS/INDPRO acceleration signal versus 20-day forward returns is not statistically distinguishable from the IC using RSAFS or INDPRO alone (p > 0.10 on interaction term).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where PAYEMS monthly growth decelerates below its trailing 12-month average while UNRATE remains near cycle lows, the labor market signals peak employment conditions, foreshadowing a slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter. In early-cycle recoveries the same deceleration does not carry this bearish signal.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average monthly change falls below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9 indicating tight labor market
failure_condition: The bearish signal fails to outperform a random baseline in late-cycle windows, defined as forward 60d returns not being significantly lower (t-test p > 0.05) than unconditional average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in INDPRO reflect improving industrial output and corporate production capacity, which supports near-term earnings momentum and generates modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week. This is a continuous always-on trend-following effect.
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS rises above 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to meaningfully compress equity valuation multiples and increase recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until rates decline below this threshold.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest value 1.91 well below threshold
failure_condition: Conditional on FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, mean forward 20d SPX returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) across observed historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing expansion regime, sustained increases in HOUST (housing starts) signal robust consumer demand and construction-linked economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. In a housing downturn regime, the same metric loses predictive power because starts reflect a lagging contraction already priced in.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and 60-day forward returns is not significantly higher during expansion-labeled periods than during contraction-labeled periods (difference not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a stable or falling FEDFUNDS implies a deteriorating real interest rate (negative real yield trend), which encourages near-term risk-asset speculation and produces short-horizon positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. Neither inflation nor policy rate alone captures this real-rate compression effect.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rising while FEDFUNDS is flat or declining
regime_hint: negative or falling real interest rate environment
failure_condition: The joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS real-rate proxy shows no significant improvement in Rank IC over the FEDFUNDS-only baseline (interaction term p > 0.10) for 5-day forward returns.
